notes.anchor: to send our Cicero to meet Caesar, but we have changed the plan
notes.note: The same scheme floated in Fam. 14.11 to send young Marcus as an intercessor to the victorious Caesar; it was dropped because no firm news of Caesar's return to Italy had reached Brundisium in mid-47 BC.
Sicca was a trusted friend and confidential agent of Cicero who had sheltered him at Vibo during the exile of 58 BC; here he again serves as the bearer of news too sensitive to commit to writing.
